data.title: MANTRA: CEX's reckless liquidation during the period of low liquidity caused OM to plummet, which may be due to negligence or market manipulation
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News Layer 1 blockchain project MANTRA published an article in its official blog to officially respond to its "OM token crash": "It has been determined that the volatility of the OM market was caused by the reckless forced liquidation of OM account holders initiated by centralized exchanges. The timing and depth of the crash indicate that account positions were closed very suddenly without adequate warning or notification. This happened during the low liquidity hours in the early morning of Asian time, which at least shows that there is a certain degree of negligence on the part of centralized exchanges, or perhaps deliberate market manipulation. Centralized exchange partners play an important role in providing liquidity for projects like ours. We work closely with them, however, they still have great discretion. When this discretion is exercised without proper internal and external supervision, market dislocations may occur, as has recently occurred, to the detriment of the interests of the project and investors. It should be clear that this market dislocation was not caused by the team, the MANTRA Chain Association, its core advisors, or MANTRA's investors selling tokens. The tokens are still locked and subject to the announced vesting period. OM Token economics remain unchanged, as we stated in our latest token report last week. Our token wallet addresses are online and visible.”

data.title: CZ responds to OM flash crash: Don’t chase narratives, CEX should no longer have a listing process, and investors should decide on their own trading products
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News CZ posted a message on the X platform after OM crashed this morning, advising investors, "Don't chase narratives. Stick to basic principles, projects with users, income, and profits."
In response to the community's question about "OM crash, whether Binance has done due diligence on it", CZ once again emphasized that CEX should no longer have a listing process, and investors should decide on their own trading products.

data.title: DePIN project Solix DePIN completes $29.5 million financing, led by Eclip Foundation
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily reported that DePIN startup Solix announced the completion of a $29.5 million financing led by Eclip Foundation. This round of funds will be used to accelerate the development of its MODEL CONTEXT PROTOCOL (MCP) technology, which enables intelligent bandwidth sharing without affecting the user's Internet experience.
Solix allows users to share idle bandwidth through browser extensions and receive rewards. It currently covers 63 countries around the world and processes an average of 275TB of data per day. Its MCP protocol dynamically allocates bandwidth through real-time contextual data, taking into account both network performance and user experience, and is considered an important technological breakthrough in the DePIN track.

data.title: Binance users targeted in SMS phishing scam using fake security alerts
data.description:
Phishing scammers have targeted Binance users in a new campaign using text messages. According to reports from users of the exchanges, the scammers are sending messages that look genuine. Text messages shared by users follow a similar pattern. They call attention to a security issue and require the user to call a number to resolve […]
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/binance-users-targeted-in-sms-phishing-scam/

data.title: Bitcoin (BTC) Bulls Push Through 50-Day SMA, Price Ready For $94K
data.description:
Bitcoin (BTC) price has surged through the 50-day Simple Moving Average (SMA), signaling a continuation of its bullish momentum. The next major resistance lies at the 200-day SMA, located around $87,250. A breakout above this resistance level could pave the way for Bitcoin to reach $94,100
source: https://www.thecoinrepublic.com/2025/04/13/bitcoin-btc-bulls-push-through-50-day-sma-price-ready-for-94k/

data.title: Commerce Secretary Lutnick walks back tariff relief on electronics
data.description:
Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick walked back the recent reciprocal tariff exemption on select electronics announced in an April 12 bulletin from the United States Customs and Border Protection. On April 13, Lutnick told ABC News that the reciprocal tariff exemption was temporary until the administration established a sector tariff regime for semiconductor products, which includes phones, graphics processors, and computing chips in a "month or two. " Lutnick added:"President Trump has called out pharmaceuticals, semiconductors, and autos. He called them sector tariffs, and those are not available for negotiation
source: https://cointelegraph.com/news/commerce-secretary-lutnick-walks-back-tariff-relief-electronics?utm_source=rss_feed&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=rss_partner_inbound
